threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You mention getting your coffee from Pret while standing in line at the local bakery. The side-eye you'll get is priceless.
Last week on the 333, a guy serenaded the whole bus with old soul tracks. Never thought I'd see Brixton's night life spill over like that!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a hipster on a bike dodging a street vendor selling traditional Turkish gözleme—only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those spontaneous outdoor chess games? In Friedrichshain parks, they turn into local exhibitions with crowds cheering every brilliant move.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they know to avoid the 24th St BART at 5 PM like it’s a zombie apocalypse, not just rush hour.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw an artist on the 31 Balboa, sketching the faces of sleeping passengers. The bus turned into a gallery for a brief moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think they can just walk into the local bars and expect to be served right away. Here, a friendly smile and a moment of patience go a long way.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their puzzled looks at Gràcia's winding streets; they still think GPS can guide them through the maze.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when a bus driver shouts 'buses only!' at pedestrians. It’s either that or you think it's a warning.
Last night, a bloke on the 36 bus swore he spotted a badger roaming near the Bussey Building. Only in Peckham, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street musician playing a theremin outside the supermarket while a toddler danced to the sound of sirens. That's Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a full bar inside an abandoned subway station, complete with a DJ spinning under the flickering lights of old transit signage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they navigate the alleys—swiftly dodging those wild street art battles while still holding a burrito.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver serenading passengers with an old jazz tune. For a moment, it felt like we were all in the same rhythm, city’s heartbeat.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that many Gràcia cafes close for a few hours in the afternoon. They think it's just siesta vibes, but it’s a chance to reset before the evening bustle.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at the time it takes for a metro train to arrive on L2, even during rush hour.
